channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'London Falling': A teenage imposter, an aging gangster and a body in the Thames
item.description: In 2019, 19-year-old Zac Brettler leapt towards the River Thames from a fifth-floor luxury apartment in central London. Patrick Radden Keefe investigates the story of the teen's double life in a new book.
item.content:encoded: In 2019, 19-year-old Zac Brettler leapt towards the River Thames from a fifth-floor luxury apartment in central London. Patrick Radden Keefe investigates the story of the teen's double life in a new book.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: Opinion: Humanity's hopes ascended with Artemis II
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon reflects on the successful launch of NASA's Artemis II this week. The four astronauts aboard will travel around the moon.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Scott Simon reflects on the successful launch of NASA's Artemis II this week. The four astronauts aboard will travel around the moon.

(Image credit: Chris O'Meara)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Iran war enters its 6th week as military searches for downed jet crew member
item.description: The war in Iran enters its 6th week as the search continues for the missing U.S. service member who bailed out of a fighter jet shot down over Iran on Friday.
item.content:encoded:
The war in Iran enters its 6th week as the search continues for the missing U.S. service member who bailed out of a fighter jet shot down over Iran on Friday.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: The busiest place you've never seen
item.description: Photographer Julia Gunther and writer-filmmaker Nick Schönfeld chronicle the rhythms of daily life on Tristan da Cunha, the world's most remote inhabited island.
item.content:encoded:
Photographer Julia Gunther and writer-filmmaker Nick Schönfeld chronicle the rhythms of daily life on Tristan da Cunha, the world's most remote inhabited island.

(Image credit: Nick Schönfeld for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Julia Gunther

item.title: Buttercream wool and jelly bean eyes: The art of the Easter lamb cake
item.description: The cakes – usually baked in the shape of a lamb using a special pan – have a long history in Central Europe, from the German osterlamm, to the Polish baranek wielkanocny, to the Alsatian lammele.
item.content:encoded:
The cakes – usually baked in the shape of a lamb using a special pan – have a long history in Central Europe, from the German osterlamm, to the Polish baranek wielkanocny, to the Alsatian lammele.

(Image credit: Charra Jarosz)
item.dc:creator: Deena Prichep

item.title: When legal sports betting surges, so do Americans' financial problems
item.description: As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.
item.content:encoded:
As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.

(Image credit: Charlie Riedel)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Congress gave money for global HIV work. The Trump administration isn't spending it
item.description: U.S. work combatting HIV/AIDS has saved millions of lives globally. Under the Trump administration, funding has been slow in coming and unpredictable, wreaking havoc on people trying to do the work.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. work combatting HIV/AIDS has saved millions of lives globally. Under the Trump administration, funding has been slow in coming and unpredictable, wreaking havoc on people trying to do the work.

(Image credit: Ben de la Cruz/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Dr. Sanjay Gupta explains what we do — and still don't — know about pain
item.description: "Pain is a mysterious thing," says neurosurgeon Dr. Sanjay Gupta. But understanding how it works in the body and different kinds of treatment can help you find the right pain relief when you need it.
item.content:encoded: "Pain is a mysterious thing," says neurosurgeon Dr. Sanjay Gupta. But understanding how it works in the body and different kinds of treatment can help you find the right pain relief when you need it.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Tax refunds are trending a bit higher this year. Here's how people are spending them
item.description: Some people are splurging. Others are finding that their refunds are being swallowed up by the rising cost of gas.
item.content:encoded:
Some people are splurging. Others are finding that their refunds are being swallowed up by the rising cost of gas.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: 65, single, seeking a roommate: More seniors are being priced out of living alone
item.description: Roommates overall are skewing older, as young people stay with their parents for longer. The share of older adults looking to rent with a roommate has tripled from a decade ago.
item.content:encoded: Roommates overall are skewing older, as young people stay with their parents for longer. The share of older adults looking to rent with a roommate has tripled from a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden
